text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Yuen
MRN: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Oncology/Interventional Radiology]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Thoracic Oncology / Interventional Radiology
PATHOLOGIST: [Staff Pathologist, Surgical Pathology]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass (3 cores)
  B. CT-guided core needle biopsy, right paratracheal lymph node (station 4R) (2 cores)

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [See header]
DATE OF REPORT: [See header]
CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known Stage IIIB (now Stage IV, M1b) non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R mutant, on osi (Tagrisso) therapy x 14 months. Re-biopsy requested for molecular re-profiling at time of radiographic progression: enlarging right upper lobe mass (1.8 cm -> 2.7 cm) and new right paratracheal lymphadenopathy. Patient previously received stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) for three intracranial metastases approximately 6 weeks prior to this procedure. Biopsies obtained to guide selection of subsequent systemic therapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS:
   INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA, consistent with known primary lung adenocarcinoma.
   - Morphologically similar to prior biopsy (Surgical Pathology [prior accession]).
   - Immunohistochemistry: TTF-1 positive (diffuse, strong); Napsin-A positive.
   - EGFR mutational analysis (rapid PCR, cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2): EGFR L858R
     mutation CONFIRMED (exon 21).
   - EGFR T790M (exon 20): NOT DETECTED by PCR (below assay detection threshold
     of approximately 1% allele frequency).
   - See Comment.
   - Comprehensive genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx) ordered; results PENDING
     (see separate report when finalized).

B. CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT PARATRACHEAL LYMPH NODE (STATION 4R):
   METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, consistent with lung primary.
   - Morphology and immunophenotype (TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A positive, CK7 positive,
     CK20 negative) consistent with metastatic lung adenocarcinoma.
   - No small cell or squamous differentiation identified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right Upper Lobe Mass): The morphology of the current biopsy is closely similar to the original diagnostic biopsy performed at initial presentation. The tumor retains acinar and lepidic architectural features with columnar cells, moderate nuclear pleomorphism, and prominent nucleoli. There is no histologic transformation to small cell carcinoma or squamous cell carcinoma. Rapid PCR-based EGFR testing confirms retention of the L858R driver mutation. EGFR T790M is NOT detected by this assay; however, given the sensitivity limitations of PCR-based platforms at low allele frequencies, a low-level T790M clone (below 1% allele frequency) cannot be excluded. Concurrent liquid biopsy (cell-free DNA, Guardant360) previously detected EGFR T790M at allele frequency 0.6%, which may reflect tumor heterogeneity and/or circulating tumor DNA from spatially distinct subclones not represented in this core biopsy sample. Comprehensive tissue NGS (FoundationOne CDx) has been submitted to further characterize the resistance genomic landscape, including assessment for MET amplification, PIK3CA/PTEN alterations, and other mechanisms of acquired resistance to third-generation EGFR TKI therapy. Results are pending.

Correlation with the liquid biopsy findings and the results of comprehensive tissue NGS is strongly recommended for treatment planning.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "right upper lobe mass biopsy." Three tan-white cylindrical core needle biopsy cores are present, measuring 0.6 cm, 0.7 cm, and 0.8 cm in length, each approximately 1.0 mm in diameter. The cores are soft to firm in consistency with a tan-white to pale yellow cut surface. Cores are submitted entirely in one cassette, wrapped in paper.

Specimen B: Received in formalin, labeled "right paratracheal lymph node biopsy." Two tan-white cylindrical core needle biopsy cores are present, measuring 0.5 cm and 0.6 cm in length, each approximately 1.0 mm in diameter. The tissue appears firm with focal areas of pale tan discoloration. Cores are submitted entirely in one cassette, wrapped in paper.

All material submitted for processing.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right Upper Lobe Mass): Sections show viable malignant epithelial cells arranged in acinar and focally lepidic patterns within a desmoplastic and fibrotic stroma. Individual tumor cells are columnar to cuboidal with vesicular to hyperchromatic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are present (estimated 4-6/10 high power fields). Focal intraluminal mucin secretion is identified. There is no evidence of necrosis. No squamous pearls or intercellular bridges are seen. No Azzopardi effect (nuclear molding, crush artifact) or evidence of neuroendocrine morphology is present. The stromal background shows prominent fibrosis consistent with prior treatment effect. Areas of hyalinized stroma interspersed with viable tumor nests are appreciated, consistent with partial response to prior EGFR-targeted therapy.

Specimen B (Right Paratracheal Lymph Node): Sections show fragmented lymphoid tissue with effacement of normal nodal architecture by sheets and nests of malignant epithelial cells with morphology identical to Specimen A. The malignant cells are embedded within a fibrous stroma. No necrosis is identified. No small cell or sarcomatoid transformation is seen.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Specimen A):
  - TTF-1 (SP141 clone): POSITIVE -- diffuse, strong nuclear staining in tumor cells
  - Napsin-A: POSITIVE -- diffuse granular cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells
  - CK7: POSITIVE (diffuse)
  - CK20: NEGATIVE
  - p40: NEGATIVE
  - Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE
  - Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE
  - CD56: NEGATIVE
  Interpretation: Immunophenotype supports adenocarcinoma of lung origin. No evidence
  of small cell or squamous histologic transformation.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Specimen B):
  - TTF-1: POSITIVE (diffuse, strong)
  - Napsin-A: POSITIVE
  - CK7: POSITIVE
  - CK20: NEGATIVE
  Interpretation: Metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with lung primary.

MOLECULAR / SOMATIC MUTATION TESTING (Specimen A):
  - EGFR mutational analysis (cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2, Roche Diagnostics):
      EGFR Exon 21 L858R:  DETECTED (positive)
      EGFR Exon 20 T790M:  NOT DETECTED (below assay limit of detection)
      EGFR Exon 19 deletion:  NOT DETECTED
      EGFR Exon 20 insertion:  NOT DETECTED
  Note: Assay sensitivity approximately 1.0% allele frequency for T790M under
  current FFPE tissue conditions. Low-level T790M below this threshold cannot
  be excluded. Liquid biopsy results (Guardant360, performed separately) should
  be reviewed in conjunction.

COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING:
  - FoundationOne CDx (Foundation Medicine): ORDERED -- results PENDING.
    Expected turnaround: 10-14 business days.
    Testing includes: SNVs, indels, copy number alterations (including MET
    amplification), fusions (ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK, MET exon 14), TMB, MSI status.

ALK IHC (D5F3 CDx, Ventana): Not repeated on current specimen (previously negative
  on original biopsy; no clinical indication for repeat based on morphology).

PD-L1 IHC (22C3 pharmDx, Dako): PENDING -- ordered per requesting oncologist for
  reassessment of PD-L1 TPS in the setting of acquired resistance. Results to follow
  in addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Right upper lobe mass, CT-guided core needle biopsy:
   INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA of lung (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+)
   EGFR L858R: DETECTED
   EGFR T790M: NOT DETECTED by PCR (see Comment regarding assay sensitivity)
   Comprehensive genomic profiling: PENDING
   PD-L1 TPS (22C3): PENDING

B. Right paratracheal lymph node (station 4R), CT-guided core needle biopsy:
   METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, consistent with lung primary (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED BY: [Staff Pathologist, Surgical Pathology Division]
REPORT STATUS: FINAL (Ancillary studies partially pending; addendum to follow)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
